title: forsteronia affinis
intro: Forsteronia affinis, also known as the 'guanabira' or 'marijuana tree' due to its psychoactive properties, is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects. The plant's health benefits include relief from pain, fever reduction, and treatment of various skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Forsteronia affinis typically involve the roots, which can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, allowing for the extraction of its bioactive compounds.
